displayName: Example — recipe-driven two-tier control band (warn+action, NOT live)
description: DEMO/EXAMPLE ONLY (next/examples/, not loaded by it-edge, not bound to any source). Shows the two-tier control-limit pattern: a measured process value must stay inside the recipe band, with a WARNING when it leaves the inner Warngrenze band and an ERROR/action alarm when it leaves the outer Eingriffsgrenze band (op=within_limits, discrepancyClass=control_limit). The literal limits below make the demo self-contained; in production the band is recipe-sourced via valueFrom 'recipe:<param>' (resolves to { warn:[lo,hi], action:[lo,hi] }) and differs per article. Deliberately NOT under next/profiles/ and bound to no source, so it never makes anything un-evaluable live.

attributes.name: Act_State_Running
attributes.description: Machine is running (precondition for the band check).

attributes.name: article_ref
attributes.description: Produced article (binding axis for the active ProductDefinition).

relationships.description: The tolerance band of the rule below is resolved from the active ProductDefinition (article_ref x equipment x setup).

temp_z1_within_control_limits.description: Zone-1 temperature must stay within the two-tier control band: warning when it leaves the inner Warngrenze band, action/error when it leaves the outer Eingriffsgrenze band. Self-contained literal limits here; in production recipe-sourced via valueFrom 'recipe:temp_z1_band'.

isa95.note: Example process entity (ACTUAL) whose tolerance band comes from the ProductDefinition (SOLL) as a two-tier warn/action control limit. DEMO ONLY — not canonical, bound to no source.
